author: @chriskelly4619
text: Rovert and Paul Thank you for how you help people. I have a challenge that takes collective brain power of Good folks like you and your friends. Can we come up with an air purifier for killing and or cleaning mold spores and VOCs from mold out of indoor air  pollution. My smoke from my rock stove heating the house? I thought maybe electrostatic air purifier from microwave parts and activated carbon charcoal filter Robert Murry style diy  with computer fan. I'm poor and sickly (moldy house) in a small house i need fresh air from outside.its cold and wet like england where i live. Look forward to hearing from everyone.

replies.author: @AndreaDingbatt
replies.text:
It sounds like you already have a great idea on the way!!
My absolute sympathy btw as I live in an older damp flat in the UK also!!
If you are in a housing association/council house/Flat..
There's a scheme for the problem that you have..
If you are a private tenant or own the house,then I think that you may be eligible for a grant to sort this out?!
Google can be a good place to start.
My Kindest Regards, and Best Wishes,
I hope you are able to resolve your issue ASAP!!
Andrea and Critters. ..XxX...
Ps, I'm sorry I maybe read your comment wrong, and thought you live in the UK?!!
So I apologise if I have given you useless information?!

author: @nunyabidniz2868
text: Thanks for the kind offer of the plans! Just might want to correct the "fuel shoot" typo to "fuel *chute*" before you get too many of them sent off [those sorts of mistakes can become so embarrassing once they get out in the wild!] 😉

author: @InfinityMindDIY
text: This is a J Stove not a Rocket Stove, Rocket stoves use a cooling surface to cool the exhaust and draw more O2 into the system, this J stove creates its draw by using the principle that heat rises. Both are cool but work differently.

author: @bikerfirefarter7280
text: Rob, ffs, as a 'potter' you should know more about combustion practicalities than to tout this iron kludge as a 'rocket stove'. For a start wood/fuel varies so much, this design is inefficient and certainly not 'fool-proof'. The average person these days are clue-less about any solid fuel or how to start/maintain 'fire' let alone make practical use of it. Most don't even have the sense to avoid traffic. Few could access/prepare feed-stock without maiming themselves. And a tall design like that is an incipient accident/booby-trap.     Stop.    I have no idea where you are going with this trivia.    No offense, but wtf?  Peace, respect, but ???

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: oh mate - you are off on one I think? You know as well as I do there is no such thing as fool proof- it doesn't matter what you do someone, somewhere at sometime is going to do something dumb - as for not a rocket stove' I disagree -but them I am not selling this - the choice is really yours as to what you do isn't it?

author: @DavidKenny64
text: If I could make one suggestion to Paul, it would be to raise the support legs in the back up to the bottom of the rest of the body.  And then to add an extension at each end down to the previous level, matching the leg in the low part of the front tube.  The way it currently designed assumes it will be sitting on a flat surface, which may cause problems outside and even inside in some cases.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nice suggestion mate and good thinking

author: @alangknowles
text: Chute not shoot.

replies.author: @paulbirbeck
replies.text: Of course it is! I put it down to an age-related brain fart. 🤭

author: @SundogbuildersNet
text:
Good on you for providing these plans! These are really great for portable cooking and I highly recommend them.

Word of warning however: DO NOT attempt to convert these to a home heating solution. That is an entirely different kettle of fish and this particular stove design is  unsuitable for that job.

author: @preciousplasticph
text: You should look into my hybrid tlud/rocket stove design on my channel.  It solves the issue of smoke when the rocket stove gets a excess fuel feed, by having a secondary combustion at the top.  You could also design a mod to.your design that ads air to the top for a secondary combustion or if you wanted to get really clever, use the guts from a catalytic converter on your stove.
